feat: intelligent invoice generation system

  and show dismissible AI-generated banner when items are auto-filled

  from a submission card, with loading state (⏳) on the button

  access control (clientId === user.id)

- Add branded HTML invoice email sent via Nodemailer when admin transitions
  invoice status to SENT, with CharaTech purple gradient header, itemised
  table, totals, notes and CTA button

- Rewrite client dashboard to include Invoices tab alongside Submissions:
  invoice table with status badges, overdue highlighting, inline view modal
  with glassmorphism styling

feat(frontend): Memoize derived dependencies in `UserSnapshotWorker` (#11898)

# Motivation

The `UserSnapshotWorker`'s `$effect` was triggering on every granular
store update (per-token balance changes, per-transaction data updates),
causing excessive timer resets and snapshot calls. The signals needed to
be coarsened so the effect only fires when meaningful state transitions
occur.

# Changes

- Replace `$balancesStore` dependency with `$anyBalanceNonZero` — a
memoized boolean that flips only when the overall balance status
changes, not on every per-token update.
- Replace the four individual transaction store dependencies
(`$btcTransactionsStore`, `$ethTransactionsStore`,
`$icTransactionsStore`, `$solTransactionsStore`) with a single
`transactionTokenEntryCount` signal built via `derivedMemo`, which emits
only when a new token's transactions appear, not on data updates within
existing entries.
- The periodic timer still captures ongoing transaction and balance
changes between snapshots.

# Tests

Current tests should work.

commit.message:
Fix lazy import of jq to avoid ModuleNotFoundError at import time

The 'Remove deps on jq' commit (3e801df) removed jq from ivpm.yaml/pyproject.toml
but left a top-level 'import jq' in eval_jq.py, which caused ModuleNotFoundError
when the module was imported in environments without jq installed.

Move the import inside the function so it only fails when jq is actually used.

commit.message:
Fix flaky replication-multiinstance test with retry-based state comparison (#8031)

* Initial plan

* Fix flaky replication-multiinstance test by using retry-based state comparison

Replace ensureEqualState with awaitCollectionsHaveEqualState which retries
the comparison with exponential backoff until state converges or times out.
This handles the inherent timing issues in multi-instance replication where
documents may not have fully propagated across instances when the comparison
runs.
